mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate level raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing forward valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS levels are associated with lower subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns as financing costs restrain corporate investment and consumer credit.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns is not negative and statistically significant (p>0.10) over any rolling 3-year window, or periods of high FEDFUNDS coincide with above-median forward returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor markets, evidenced by unemployment rising sharply while payrolls contract simultaneously, signals an accelerating demand shock that forces near-term equity repricing lower. This joint labor deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 150k in the same month
failure_condition: When trigger is active, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional 5-day return distribution (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling while the federal funds rate remains elevated jointly indicate that tight monetary policy is actively suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand. This combined signal - low HOUST paired with high FEDFUNDS - anticipates a broader demand slowdown that depresses S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, beyond what either series predicts alone.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with weakening housing demand
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST decline x FEDFUNDS level) shows no incremental predictive power over either univariate proxy (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) across a 3-year rolling walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Following a recessionary trough, an inflection in industrial production growth from negative to positive signals the start of a manufacturing-led recovery, which historically precedes broad equity market gains. INDPRO turning positive after a sustained contraction predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month, but only when the economy is emerging from a recession.
failure_condition: In post-recession regimes identified by prior GDPC1 contraction, INDPRO inflections fail to produce positive 20d forward returns more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over the recovery sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and drives modest but consistent positive equity drift over the following trading week. RSAFS growth trend is treated as an always-on positive signal for short-horizon returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with trailing 3-month RSAFS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A turn in nonfarm payrolls from consecutive monthly declines to positive growth marks the start of a labor market recovery, which historically leads a sustained equity rally over the subsequent quarter as employment income supports consumption and earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS posts first positive month-over-month change after at least 3 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Following the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the average post-trigger return is not significantly greater than the unconditional 60-day return mean (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime where headline CPI growth is decelerating, easing price pressure reduces the probability of further monetary tightening, lowering the equity risk premium and supporting forward S&P 500 returns over one month. The effect is regime-dependent: it only holds when inflation is already on a declining trajectory rather than accelerating.
regime_hint: disinflationary regime with restrictive policy rate
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed CPIAUCSL deceleration (3-month annualized rate falling), 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive relative to unconditional baseline (p>0.10), or the effect reverses sign in more than one 2-year subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and rising unemployment jointly confirm an active recessionary demand shock, amplifying downside risk to corporate earnings beyond what either series signals independently. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter until GDP growth stabilizes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth negative AND UNRATE rising for 3+ consecutive months
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions hold jointly, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the joint interaction term adds no explanatory power over the univariate GDPC1 or UNRATE signals (incremental R^2 < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.
